description: Reviewed URDF and MJCF import cases that pin the supported deterministic subset and fail-closed numeric and graph behavior.

cases.focus: A minimal finite URDF box remains importable as a shape-valid contract.
A minimal finite MJCF body remains importable as a shape-valid contract.
Non-finite URDF poses never silently become the origin.

input.xml: <robot name="u"><link name="base"/><joint name="j" type="fixed"><parent link="base"/><child link="tip"/><origin xyz="NaN 0 0"/></joint><link name="tip"/></robot>

cases.focus: Infinite primitive dimensions cannot reach geometry or placeholder fallback.
Malformed dimensions fail instead of silently producing a geometry-free part.
A non-finite joint axis is rejected before dominant-axis selection.

input.xml: <mujoco model="m"><worldbody><body name="base"><body name="tip"><joint type="hinge" axis="0 NaN 0" range="-1 1"/><geom type="box" size="0.1 0.1 0.1"/></body></body></worldbody></mujoco>

cases.focus: An import with no supported skeleton nodes is refused.
Every imported link must retain an explicit stable identity.
Imported joint graphs cannot reference absent parents.

input.xml: <robot name="u"><link name="tip"></link><joint name="j" type="fixed"><parent link="ghost"/><child link="tip"/></joint></robot>

cases.focus: Duplicate imported node identities are refused before contract assembly.
A joint cannot disappear merely because its declared child link is absent.

input.xml: <robot name="u"><link name="base"/><joint name="j" type="fixed"><parent link="base"/><child link="ghost"/></joint></robot>

cases.focus: A cyclic parent graph cannot be assembled into an apparently valid skeleton.

input.xml: <robot name="u"><link name="a"></link><link name="b"></link><joint name="ab" type="fixed"><parent link="a"/><child link="b"/></joint><joint name="ba" type="fixed"><parent link="b"/><child link="a"/></joint></robot>
